hypothesis: Incorporating structural diversity among ensemble members—through heterogeneous GNN architectures (e.g., combining PaiNN, SchNet, and DimeNet) rather than identical architectures with different random initializations—within the ensemble consensus semi-supervised learning framework will yield greater ambiguity (prediction variance) as formalized in the bias-variance decomposition, and consequently produce superior individual member performance and ensemble accuracy compared to homogeneous ensembles of equivalent size, particularly for molecular properties that depend on different geometric or electronic features captured differentially by distinct architectural inductive biases.
